panels.title: Messages delivered per chamber (recipient view)
panels.description: Count of messages delivered TO each chamber's mailman over time. Source: mailman log lines matching `delivering id=` from `tmux-tell-claude-mailman@<chamber>.service` (tight selector excludes gate_max_wait retry-warnings which also contain the word `delivering`). The `agent` label IS the recipient.

panels.title: Messages sent per chamber (sender view)
panels.description: Count of messages sent BY each chamber. Parsed from `from=<sender>` in the mailman log line. Asymmetries vs. the recipient view above surface broadcast-only or listen-only patterns.

panels.title: Body bytes delivered per chamber
panels.description: Sum of `body_bytes=<N>` per recipient. Distinguishes high-volume-tiny-messages (lots of acks) from low-volume-large-messages (long dispatches).

targets.expr: sum by (agent) (sum_over_time({unit=~"tmux-tell-claude-mailman@.+\\.service"} |~ "delivering id=" | regexp `body_bytes=(?P<bytes>\d+)` | unwrap bytes [$__interval]))

panels.title: Warning + abort events
panels.description: Mailman-side delivery warnings: `gate_max_wait` (deliver-anyway after 5m gate-state-unknown), `pre_paste_safety_abort` (popup-suspected / probe-failed; re-queue for retry). Rising rates indicate delivery-stall trouble.

panels.title: Recent traffic tail
panels.description: Live log feed combining real deliveries + retry warnings. Real deliveries match `delivering id=<id> kind=<kind> from=<sender> body_bytes=<N>`; retry warnings (gate_max_wait, pre_paste_safety_abort) also contain the word `delivering`. The broader `delivering` filter is INTENTIONAL here — surfacing stuck-message retries inline (e.g., a message hitting gate_max_wait every ~5min for hours) gives the "what's stuck" view this panel is designed for. The numeric panels above (10/11/20) use the tighter `delivering id=` to exclude these warnings from delivery counts.
